summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates AWS credentials and Secrets Manager contents to hardcoded attacker IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a container/CI-targeted credential stealer. It checks the host's name and current working directory, bailing out when the hostname contains 'ubuntu', 'hetzner', or 'nijin' or when the cwd is under /tmp/, and firing only when the hostname matches a 12-hex Docker container ID pattern or cwd contains /app/. When the gate passes, it shells out via curl to the AWS instance metadata service (169.254.169.254) using IMDSv2, extracts AccessKeyId/SecretAccessKey/SessionToken from the attached IAM role, runs `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` and `get-secret-value` to dump every accessible secret, and scans /app, /root, and /flag for HTB{...} strings. The collected JSON is PUT to http://127.0.0.1:30782 and http://154.57.164.71:30782. The hostname/cwd gate is evasion logic that suppresses execution on the author's development machines while ensuring the payload runs in victim CI and container environments.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
